results.title: Are you working on a Phantom Story? – Nick Macari
results.content:
Now if the plot changes…

If Arthur wins the sword in a duel. If he decides to only date Gwenevyre. If he chooses another knight over sir Lancelot as his best. The plot has changed, but the story itself remains.

We could showcase this even further by changing the Arthur to Annie. We could make her queen instead of king. We could change Excalibur to a spear and a host of other events and details… and still retain the essence of the True Story.

Still not convinced? [...] Still not convinced?

What if I told you I’m working on a sci-fi story where a young alien boy lives on a planet during a great time of conflict between tribes. The boy takes possession of a legendary laser weapon—a weapon no other alien is able to take possession of. One that is prophesied to find its way to the king of the planet. With the help of a magical seer the alien uses the weapon to slay a horrible beast, unite his people and become king of his planet.

I could keep going, but at some point you’re bound to throw your hands up and say something like “Nick, are you doing some sort of alien King Arthur?”   The plot has changed, the story has stayed the same.

Right now I’m working on a project where the creator delivered a five page treatment of plot. [...] A True Story is larger then its plot.

A True Story could be told many different ways. It’s details can be changed around a bit, yet the story, the essence reamins intact. In contrast a plot is set. Changing the details of a plot, creates… a new plot. (Of course most elements of a story are intimately intertwined. You can easily break a story or force a story to change by drastically altering the plot.)

Take the concept we used above:

The simple boy who becomes king.

We all know the plot, the expression of the story:

The boy draws Excalibur. He marries Gwenevyre. Teams up with Merlin. Forms the knights of the round table. And goes on to unite a land and become king of Britannia. A story about justice and morality in a dark age.

Now if the plot changes…

results.title: The Phantom Reimagined: Phantom 2040 with Alex Segura | Comics Kingdom
results.content:
# The Phantom Reimagined: Phantom 2040 with Alex Segura | Comics Kingdom
## Comics Kingdom
60600 subscribers
18 likes

### Description
748 views
Posted: 26 Sep 2025
In this episode of Inside the Kingdom, we sit down with Alex Segura (novelist, comics writer, industry veteran) to talk about Phantom 2040: A New Shadow — the bold reinvention of The Phantom launching exclusively on Comics Kingdom.

We explore:

How the modern Phantom was reimagined for a future world
The challenges and opportunities of a vertical webcomic format
Balancing legacy elements and fresh ideas
Collaboration with art, story, and technology
And what’s next for this new chapter in The Phantom’s saga

If you love comics, legacy characters, or storytelling risk-taking, this is for you. [...] doing something else that's much more exciting. Yeah. There are worse gigs, right? Um, so let's move on into into 2040. Obviously, it's launched here um as of Wednesday. Super super proud of the work. It's been wellreceived. It's an amazing piece. It's going to be an amazing series. Can't wait for fans to see, you know, Carlos actually wear the mantle take on that that whole that whole uh uh experience, right? And um I guess going back to its origins a bit. So, um 2040 obviously I I cooked up this idea of re of reimagining that, bringing it back. And remember I remember coming to you and saying hey this idea etc we want to move away from like the walker line for this story we want to do something what what what was your reaction initially from hearing it were you just like this crazy guy [...] the new Serena Carpenter as well. So it's, you know, it depends on the day. I can't get Pink Pony Club out of my head for some reason. I know. Yeah. Yeah. She was just here in Forest Hills. Yeah. Well, Alex, this of course has been fantastic. This has been always a pleasure. Um, yeah, exactly. It's always a pleasure to to link up and then obviously here now that we've launched to be out in the market and see the fans reaction and really talk through, you know, this baby that we've been we've been birthing, I guess, for the last better part of a year. Um, it's out there. Yeah. Thanks again sharing all your insights. um obviously the little tidbits of behind the scenes that we can give folks uh at this stage, but um and of course the excitement all all together on Phantom 2040. Um I can't
